claim: Application-layer AI security risks (improper RAG access controls, unsafe agent tools, API vulnerabilities) are the primary concern for production AI systems, not foundation model toxicity which vendors have largely fixed
actionable: Focus AI security efforts on your application layer—access controls, tool permissions, API exposure—rather than assuming foundation models are the bottleneck
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A

claim: AI-versus-AI red teaming generates application-specific attacks tailored to each system's business context, going beyond static vulnerability databases or generic prompt injections
actionable: Use AI-versus-AI techniques to generate dynamic, context-aware attacks rather than relying on static lists of known vulnerabilities
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A

claim: Evaluation tools are table stakes and becoming commoditized; the real value in AI security lies in systematic red teaming to find and fix application-specific risks before production
actionable: Move beyond basic evals as a commodity baseline; invest in red teaming practices that uncover risks specific to your application's business logic
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A

claim: AI security red teaming should include multi-turn attack sequences where an attacker starts with innocent questions and gradually leads the model down a vulnerable path across 10-20 back-and-forths
actionable: When testing AI security, design multi-turn jailbreak tests that gradually steer the model toward vulnerability rather than single direct attacks
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A

claim: There's a delta between foundation model incentives (maximize helpfulness) and production application incentives (don't recommend competitor products, don't suggest off-label drug use) that requires explicit testing
actionable: Test for application-specific risks where the model's training objective (helpfulness) conflicts with your business requirements
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A

claim: The remediation options for AI security findings are limited: harden prompts, tune guardrails, or fix access controls; guardrails alone are insufficient for all risk classes
actionable: When finding security issues, recognize that prompt hardening and guardrails work for prompt-based risks, but API/access control issues require architectural fixes
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A

claim: Fortune 500 companies have the most to lose from AI security issues and show strongest adoption of AI red teaming tools; over 10% of Fortune 500 are customers of Promptfoo
actionable: If selling AI security, target large enterprises with significant liability exposure; they are early adopters willing to invest in pre-deployment testing
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers are mostly being deployed as 'glorified API wrappers' by enterprises; deeper security implications around context exfiltration and untrusted servers are often missed
actionable: When implementing MCP, think beyond basic API wrapping; test for context exfiltration risks, rogue server scenarios, and tool injection attacks
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A

claim: Security tooling needs to move earlier in the SDLC (CI/CD, pre-deployment) rather than being an after-the-fact check, because agents with long-running executions require observability and white-box testing
actionable: Integrate AI security testing into CI/CD pipelines and leverage code-aware discovery rather than treating it as a post-deployment validation
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A

claim: Guardrails are becoming commoditized (most teams use the guardrails from their inference provider); the future of AI security is pre-deployment measurement and risk management, not runtime blocking alone
actionable: Don't rely on guardrails as your sole security strategy; invest in pre-deployment testing to measure risk rather than attempting to block all issues at runtime
source_title: Breaking AI to Fix It: Ian Webster's Journey from Discord's Clyde to Promptfoo's $18M Series A
